title: Korean-Style Roast Grape Leaf Wraps
description: A sweet and savory wrap filled with the flavors of Korea and the freshness of grape leaves. Perfect for a quick snack or light meal.

ingredients.name: Grape Leaves
ingredients.notes: fresh or jarred, drained and chopped

ingredients.name: Fragrant Rice
ingredients.notes: or substitute with other cooked rice

instructions: If using jarred grape leaves, rinse and drain them. If using fresh, blanch them in boiling water for 30 seconds, then chop.
In a large skillet, heat the sesame oil over medium heat. Add the chopped roast and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the Korean chili flakes and soy sauce to the skillet and stir to combine. Cook for an additional 1 minute.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked rice, green onions (if using), and cooked roast mixture. Stir to combine.
To assemble the wraps, lay a grape leaf flat on a work surface, with the stem end facing you.
Spoon about 1/4 cup of the rice mixture onto the center of the leaf, leaving a 1-inch border on either side.
Fold the stem end over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the leaf into a neat package.
Repeat with the remaining grape leaves and filling.
Serve the wraps warm, or at room temperature.
